task_completion.description: Open the initial presentation in LibreOffice Impress
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Impress window is visible with 'animation_test.odp' in the title bar and 2 slides shown in the left slide pane.
The 'Slide Transition' deck/tab is active and visible in the right sidebar of LibreOffice Impress.

task_completion.description: Apply a transition effect to a slide
task_completion.visual_evidence: A specific transition effect (e.g., Wipe, Wheel) is highlighted in the Slide Transition pane, and/or a small star icon appears next to a slide thumbnail in the left pane indicating a transition is applied.
The 'Animation' deck/tab is active and visible in the right sidebar of LibreOffice Impress.

task_completion.description: Apply an animation effect to an object on a slide
task_completion.visual_evidence: An object (text box, shape, etc.) on the slide is selected, and an animation entry (with an effect name and trigger icon) is visible in the Animation pane list.

task_completion.description: Open the Save As dialog to save the updated file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save As' dialog box is open on the screen in LibreOffice Impress.

task_completion.description: Save the presentation to the correct path and format
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save As' dialog shows the directory '/home/ga/Documents/', the file name 'presentation.pptx', and the file type set to PowerPoint (.pptx), OR the main window title bar updates to 'presentation.pptx'.

integrity.description: Agent used the LibreOffice Impress GUI to apply effects and save the file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks and navigation through the LibreOffice Impress menus and sidebars, rather than terminal commands executing scripts to modify the file.

integrity.description: Agent actually applied effects rather than just opening the panes
integrity.visual_evidence: The animation and transition lists show populated effects applied to specific slides/objects, not just empty panes.

integrity.description: Agent properly converted the file format using the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the 'Save As' dialog in Impress to select the PPTX format, rather than just renaming the file extension from .odp to .pptx in a file manager or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial file is located at /home/ga/Documents/Presentations/animation_test.odp and contains 2 slides. The task requires using LibreOffice Impress to add both slide transitions and object animations. The final file must be saved exactly as /home/ga/Documents/presentation.pptx. Note: Look closely at the Impress sidebars for the star icons and animation list entries to verify the effects were actually added.
